payload.description: New Metro-North link to Penn Station providing one-seat passenger rail service for Metro-North's New Haven Line customers, running through the Bronx via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line, entering Queens via the Hell Gate Bridge, and connecting to the LIRR Mainline at Harold Interlocking to access Penn Station. Includes construction of four new stations in the Bronx (Hunts Point, Parkchester-Van Nest, Morris Park, Co-op City), two new substations in the Bronx, two new substations in Queens (48th Street and Gate), and other right-of-way augmentations.

Acquisition of property at 3876-3880 Park Avenue, Bronx, NY for a new electrical substation in support of Metro-North operations on the Harlem Line. Property currently contains a vacant three-story industrial building to be demolished for substation construction.

Long-term ground leasing and development of a Development Site at 1824 Allerton Avenue, The Bronx for a new multi-story warehouse and distribution facility (approx. 2,188,985 sq ft) including an approx. 286,367 sq ft NYCT Facility for future use as an electric bus charging facility. 99-year ground lease term. Developer: Madison Capital HoldCo III LLC.
